task: Write a Python function that validates AWS S3 bucket names according to AWS naming rules (3-63 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, must start and end with letter or number, no consecutive hyphens)
key_criteria_for_evaluation: Function correctly validates all AWS S3 bucket naming constraints, includes clear variable names, handles edge cases (empty strings, mixed case, special characters), and returns boolean with optional error message

task: Create a JSON IAM policy document that grants read-only access to a specific S3 bucket (GetObject, ListBucket actions) for a principal in the same AWS account
key_criteria_for_evaluation: Policy includes required Version, Statement with correct Effect, Action, and Resource fields, uses appropriate ARN syntax with wildcards where applicable, and follows AWS IAM policy best practices

task: Write a regular expression that extracts the AWS account ID (12 digits) and resource name from an S3 ARN in the format 'arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name/key-path'
key_criteria_for_evaluation: Regex correctly captures bucket name and key path components, handles ARNs with and without trailing slashes, uses appropriate grouping for extraction, and accounts for valid AWS naming constraints (hyphens, underscores)
